forms.label: Tree
forms.description: A woody plant with one main trunk or strong trunking stem.

forms.label: Shrub
forms.description: A multi-stemmed woody plant that stays lower than a tree.

forms.label: Subshrub
forms.description: A partly woody plant with soft new growth above a woody base.

forms.label: Vine
forms.description: A plant that trails, climbs, or scrambles with support.

forms.label: Herb
forms.description: A non-woody plant that puts energy into soft seasonal growth.

forms.label: Grass
forms.description: A narrow-leaved monocot with tufted or spreading growth.

forms.label: Fern
forms.description: A spore-bearing plant with divided fronds instead of true flowers.

forms.label: Bulb
forms.description: A storage organ that lets the plant rest and regrow seasonally.

forms.label: Succulent
forms.description: A water-storing plant with thick leaves, stems, or roots.

forms.label: Cactus
forms.description: A drought-adapted succulent with specialized stems and reduced leaves.

forms.label: Groundcover
forms.description: A low plant that spreads to cover soil and suppress weeds.

forms.label: Aquatic
forms.description: A plant adapted to wet edges, shallow water, or saturated soils.

forms.label: Annual
forms.description: A plant that completes its life cycle in one season.

forms.label: Biennial
forms.description: A plant that grows leaves the first year and flowers the second.

forms.label: Perennial
forms.description: A plant that returns for multiple seasons.

forms.label: Weed
forms.description: A plant growing where it is not wanted or outcompeting companions.

biology.label: Root system
biology.description: The underground system that anchors the plant and absorbs water.

biology.label: Stem structure
biology.description: The structural axis that supports leaves, flowers, and fruit.

biology.label: Leaf structure
biology.description: The photosynthetic surface and major identity signal for a plant.

biology.label: Flowering
biology.description: The reproductive display that often drives color and pollinator value.

biology.label: Fruiting
biology.description: The post-flower stage where seeds or edible structures form.

biology.label: Seed set
biology.description: The stage where new generations are packaged for dispersal.

biology.label: Pollination
biology.description: The transfer of pollen that turns bloom into seed or fruit.

biology.label: Photosynthesis
biology.description: The light-driven process that powers plant growth.

biology.label: Dormancy
biology.description: A resting phase that protects the plant during stress or cold.

biology.label: Hardiness
biology.description: The ability to survive winter, heat, or seasonal stress.

biology.label: Propagation
biology.description: How new plants are started from seed, cuttings, or division.

biology.label: Pruning
biology.description: The shape and renewal process for woody or vigorous plants.

biology.label: Transplanting
biology.description: The transition from one site or container to another.

biology.label: Fertility
biology.description: The nutrient story that keeps growth moving.

biology.label: pH balance
biology.description: The chemical balance that affects nutrient uptake.

biology.label: Water balance
biology.description: How the plant handles moisture, drainage, and drought.

biology.label: Pest pressure
biology.description: The pressure from insects and other garden pests.

biology.label: Disease pressure
biology.description: The pressure from fungal, bacterial, or viral problems.

care.label: Sunlight
care.description: Light exposure and how it powers growth.

care.label: Soil
care.description: The growing medium and its texture, drainage, and nutrient supply.

care.label: Moisture
care.description: How much water the plant wants and how fast the site dries.

care.label: Mulch
care.description: The protective top layer that conserves water and stabilizes roots.

care.label: Companion planting
care.description: Which plants grow well together and support each other.

care.label: Spacing
care.description: How much room roots and foliage need.

care.label: Deadheading
care.description: Removing spent blooms to keep the plant tidy or reblooming.

care.label: Staking
care.description: Physical support for tall or floppy stems.

care.label: Division
care.description: Propagation by separating clumps or crowns.

care.label: Compost
care.description: The slow soil-building feed that improves structure and biology.

care.label: Irrigation
care.description: The watering method that keeps the root zone stable.

care.label: Fertilizer
care.description: The nutrient plan for active growth and flowering.
